expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml; skill correctly detected and skipped install step. Dependency satisfied.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py (line 19), outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly notes 'No React frontend → no VoteFeedback, no useFeedbackState'. Signal analysis confirms 0 coded signals. No React or VoteFeedback references in diff.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: Transcript lines 164-166 confirm .env updated with KELET_PROJECT=docs-ai-assistant and KELET_API_KEY=sk-kelet-test-123. settings/__init__.py had kelet_api_key/kelet_project fields removed (SDK reads env vars directly). Keys not hardcoded in source.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md shows exactly 2 AskUserQuestion calls (Checkpoint 1 and Checkpoint 2). Well within the 3-max budget.

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Transcript: 'Coded signals: 0 (lightweight, no hooks to wire to)'. Changes limited to configure(), agentic_session(), and env vars. No unnecessary decorators, signal calls, or extra dependencies added.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution (not shown as copy-paste code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains a real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL. Transcript states 'Deeplink generated (Bash)' and shows the URL directly (not as a code block). deeplink.txt confirms actual execution.

expectations.text: Adds kelet to pyproject.toml or requirements
expectations.evidence: kelet>=1.3.0 was already in pyproject.toml (pre-existing). Transcript confirms this was detected. Dependency satisfied even without skill.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is called at startup (not per-request)
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure(api_key=settings.kelet_api_key, project=settings.kelet_project)` at module level — not per-request. The `if settings.kelet_api_key:` guard is a style choice; configure() is correctly placed at startup.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps the agent call with the existing Redis session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id):` wrapping agent call. session.session_id (the Redis UUID) is correctly used.

expectations.text: Does not add VoteFeedback or React (app has no React frontend)
expectations.evidence: Transcript notes 'Plain HTML frontend; no React/JS changes attempted' and no VoteFeedback added. Diff contains no React or VoteFeedback references.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT written to .env (not hardcoded in source)
expectations.evidence: No .env file changes in changes.diff. Transcript section 'Step 4: Problems' notes 'No env var instructions — Did not add instructions for setting KELET_API_KEY on Fly.io'. Keys are referenced via existing Settings fields but no .env was written.

expectations.text: AskUserQuestion called at most 3 times total
expectations.evidence: questions_asked.md states 'No questions were asked.' Zero AskUserQuestion calls — well within budget (though zero is arguably worse than 2 for UX quality).

expectations.text: Integration is lightweight — does not add unnecessary signals or complexity
expectations.evidence: Without_skill run added a phone_number field to ChatRequest (speculative, not requested), a user_id param, and wrapped the stateless GET /chat endpoint with a random UUID session (transcript notes this is 'likely incorrect'). Added unnecessary complexity beyond the minimal integration.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink is generated via Bash execution
expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt found in without_skill outputs. Transcript explicitly states 'Synthetic signals / evaluators — No knowledge of this feature; not implemented.'

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md documents the mismatch detection in detail. Transcript states 'Mismatch flagged: phone outlives sessions; should be user_id= not session_id'. Checkpoint 1 question explicitly surfaces this: 'Your app already generates a UUID per conversation — phone number should be passed as user_id so Kelet can correlate sessions across conversations for the same user.'

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md confirms the app already generates UUIDs correctly via create_session(). The fix proposed is to keep UUID as session_id (already correct) and add phone_number as user_id. Transcript confirms this approach explicitly.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id)` with `phone_number: str | None = None` added to ChatRequest and `user_id=body.phone_number` passed through. Phone number correctly wired as user_id.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()  # reads KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT from env` added at module level in app/main.py.

expectations.text: Surfaces the issue that phone number outlives sessions and cannot be used as session_id
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md shows the without_skill run identified the UUID-based session system in code but DID NOT surface the phone-number-as-session_id mismatch to the developer. The transcript confirms: 'Phone number / user_id: NOT handled — phone number has no path into the code, problem was abandoned'. The mismatch was recognized internally but never surfaced or resolved.

expectations.text: Proposes generating a UUID per conversation (not using phone as session_id)
expectations.evidence: session_handling.md states no UUID fix was proposed. The run simply used the existing UUID session_id silently without explaining why or acknowledging the phone-number mismatch to the developer. No explicit proposal was made.

expectations.text: Passes stable identifier (phone) as user_id= in agentic_session()
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id)` with NO user_id parameter. session_handling.md explicitly states: 'User ID: NOT handled — phone number has no path into the code, problem was abandoned'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() is still added at startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()  # reads KELET_API_KEY + KELET_PROJECT from env` added at module level in app/main.py.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: diagnosis.md correctly identifies 'Root Cause 1: Missing agentic_session()' with full explanation. transcript confirms 'Root cause diagnosis: agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id) is missing. Without it, each LLM call emits a span with no session context. Kelet cannot group them.'

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id, user_id=user_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not re-add kelet.configure() or re-ask for keys that are already present
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows kelet.configure() IS added to app/main.py even though the baseline already had kelet installed and the problem description states 'Has kelet in deps and configure() called'. The skill treated this as needing a configure() fix due to detecting kelet.init() (wrong method) in the baseline, but the assertion expects no re-adding of configure(). The transcript confirms it added configure() to fix the wrong kelet.init() method. This is a judgment call — the fix was legitimate but technically re-adds the configure call.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase V section includes: 'Smoke test: trigger POST /chat → open Kelet console → verify sessions appear (allow a few minutes)'. This is a clear verification step.

expectations.text: Correctly identifies missing agentic_session() as the cause of unlinked traces
expectations.evidence: diagnosis.md correctly identifies the root cause: 'The missing piece is kelet.agentic_session(session_id=...).' explains that configure() alone sets up the OTel pipeline but without agentic_session() each call creates an isolated root span with no gen_ai.conversation.id. transcript confirms this diagnosis was reached by reading kelet package source.

expectations.text: Adds agentic_session() wrapping the agent call
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: Does not re-add kelet.configure() or re-ask for keys that are already present
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows configure() was added to app/main.py but as part of fixing the pre-existing setup (baseline had kelet.configure() with if-guard pattern). The without_skill run preserved the existing conditional configure() guard with explicit api_key/project params and did NOT re-ask the developer for keys. questions_asked.md confirms no questions were asked.

expectations.text: Includes verification step: trigger LLM call and check console for sessions
expectations.evidence: transcript and diagnosis.md contain no verification step telling the developer how to confirm the fix worked. There is no mention of 'trigger a POST /chat and check the console' or similar smoke test guidance. The diagnosis says 'Final git diff HEAD confirmed' but that is code verification, not a runtime smoke test.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py before app creation, outside any request handler.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.agentic_session(session_id=session.session_id):` wrapping `chat_agent.iter()` in src/routers/chat.py.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows KeletProvider wrapping App in frontend/src/main.tsx with apiKey={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY} and project={import.meta.env.VITE_KELET_PROJECT}.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VoteFeedback.Root with UpvoteButton, DownvoteButton, Popover, Textarea, and SubmitButton added inside each assistant message in frontend/src/App.tsx.

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback session_id prop
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md explicitly documents the full flow: X-Session-ID header captured in React state via res.headers.get('X-Session-ID'), stored as sessionId state, passed as session_id={sessionId} to VoteFeedback.Root. Transcript verification checklist confirms 'Session ID consistent end-to-end'.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY (server) and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (frontend) both written to env
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY added to .env, but KELET_API_KEY is not in the diff (transcript notes it was already present). Only the publishable key is visibly written; secret key does not appear in the diff.

expectations.text: VoteFeedback placement and styling fits the existing chat UI theme
expectations.evidence: frontend_changes.md has a dedicated 'Theme/Style Considerations' section: Popover uses matching white background/#ccc border/blue #0070f3 submit button matching existing Send button. No style system dependency introduced.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback
expectations.evidence: Transcript explicitly states 'Lightweight mode: keep it to VoteFeedback only.' Signal analysis notes 'No copy button. No retry button. Only thing to add is VoteFeedback'. No useKeletSignal or additional signals proposed.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains real https://console.kelet.ai/docs-ai-assistant/synthetics/setup?deeplink=<base64url> URL. Transcript states 'Deeplink generated (Bash execution — not shown as code block)'.

expectations.text: kelet.configure() added at server startup
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `kelet.configure()` added at module level in app/main.py with comment '# Initialize Kelet -- reads KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT from environment'.

expectations.text: agentic_session() wraps agent call with session_id
expectations.evidence: changes.diff shows `async with kelet.session(session_id=session.session_id):` — uses kelet.session() NOT kelet.agentic_session(). Transcript documents this as 'Wrong context manager name: will likely raise AttributeError at runtime'.

expectations.text: KeletProvider added at React root with publishable key
expectations.evidence: No KeletProvider in changes.diff. frontend_changes.md: 'No client-side Kelet SDK initialization: Without knowing about the publishable key, no kelet.init() or equivalent is called in the browser.'

expectations.text: VoteFeedback component added next to AI message output
expectations.evidence: No VoteFeedback in changes.diff. frontend_changes.md: 'Since there was no skill, Claude did NOT use @kelet-ai/feedback-ui. Instead, raw feedback buttons were added directly in JSX.'

expectations.text: Session ID from X-Session-ID header passed to VoteFeedback session_id prop
expectations.evidence: Session ID is captured from X-Session-ID header correctly but there is no VoteFeedback component to pass it to. The propagation chain is broken at the VoteFeedback step.

expectations.text: KELET_API_KEY (server) and VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY (frontend) both written to env
expectations.evidence: No .env changes in changes.diff. frontend_changes.md: 'No publishable key: Without the skill, Claude doesn’t know there’s a separate publishable key (VITE_KELET_PUBLISHABLE_KEY) for frontend use.'

expectations.text: VoteFeedback placement and styling fits the existing chat UI theme
expectations.evidence: No VoteFeedback component added — assertion cannot be satisfied. Raw thumbs buttons added with no theme consideration documented.

expectations.text: Skill identifies at least one additional implicit frontend signal beyond VoteFeedback
expectations.evidence: No awareness of useKeletSignal or any Kelet frontend signal API. No client-side SDK initialized at all.

expectations.text: Synthetic evaluator deeplink generated via Bash (not shown as code block)
expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt in without_skill outputs. Transcript does not mention synthetic evaluators or deeplinks.

expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 5 explicitly lists 'Skipped (already present in baseline): kelet.configure(), kelet.agentic_session(), KELET_API_KEY and KELET_PROJECT in .env, phone_number field'. changes.diff confirms configure/agentic_session were not re-added.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md documents 4 new coded signals: agent-stream-error, session-expired-return, tool-search-error, tool-page-not-found/tool-page-error. All confirmed in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than just Task Completion
expectations.evidence: deeplink.txt contains URL with 4 evaluators: Task Completion, Sentiment Analysis, Conversation Completeness, and Role Adherence. Significantly more than just Task Completion.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: Transcript Phase 2: 'VoteFeedback decision: Not applicable. Plain HTML frontend, no React. @kelet-ai/feedback-ui is React-only. Not added.' No React code in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has 'What it catches:' subsections for each signal with specific failure modes (e.g. 'Bedrock API errors', 'Implicit session abandonment', 'Model hallucinating document slugs').

expectations.text: Does not re-add configure() or agentic_session() — skips already-done work
expectations.evidence: Transcript 'Starting state (baseline)' acknowledges both kelet.configure() and kelet.agentic_session() are already present and builds on top of them without re-adding them.

expectations.text: Proposes at least one additional coded signal beyond what's already in place
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md documents 6 signals: user_id threading, rate-limit signal, agent error signal, explicit user feedback endpoint, search no-results, get-page not-found. All implemented in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Proposes more synthetic evaluators than just Task Completion
expectations.evidence: No deeplink.txt in without_skill outputs. Transcript has no mention of synthetic evaluators or the Kelet console deeplink feature. Without the skill, there is no awareness of the synthetic evaluator setup workflow.

expectations.text: Does not propose VoteFeedback or React since app has no React frontend
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md: 'No React component possible (plain HTML), so a REST endpoint allows a simple native thumbs widget in the existing HTML frontend.' No VoteFeedback or React code in changes.diff.

expectations.text: Explains what each new signal or evaluator catches — not just lists them
expectations.evidence: signals_added.md has a 'Rationale:' section for each signal explaining the observable failure category (e.g. rate-limit: 'frustrated users who are trying to use the product but being blocked'; search no-results: 'docs don’t cover what users ask').

notes: Session boundary detection shows largest gap (+75pp on eval 2): without skill, phone-as-user-id was silently dropped
Deeplink generation is 0% discoverable without the skill (0/3 non-trivial evals generated it)
Eval 3 shows no gap (0pp) — the fix (agentic_session) is SDK-discoverable; skill value is in Phase V and avoiding over-engineering
Eval 4 React/UI integration shows massive gap (+56pp): without skill, wrong context manager, no KeletProvider, no VoteFeedback, no publishable key knowledge
Eval 5 expand-to-full shows modest gap (+20pp): without skill, signal instrumentation is largely achievable but synthetic evaluator workflow is completely unknown
no_redundant_setup assertion in eval 3 may need refinement: fixing wrong method (kelet.init → kelet.configure) was scored as failure but is correct behavior
implicit_fe_signals failed with_skill (eval 4): skill correctly applied lightweight mode but did not surface additional signal opportunities even when React frontend was present
both_keys_in_env assertion in eval 4 with_skill is borderline: KELET_API_KEY was pre-existing so not re-written; consider adjusting assertion to 'both keys present in .env at end of run'
